URL Source: https://www.techuz.com/blog/what-is-edtech-and-what-it-holds-for-education-and-startups/ Published Time: 2018-11-30T18:22:23+05:30 Markdown Content: Educational Technology or EdTech was started as an experiment in the mid-sixties by Stanford University professors. The concept here was simple \u2013 teach arithmetic and spellings to elementary school students using computers. And this was even before the internet was introduced. Almost six decades later, employing the reach of the internet, modern devices and highly sophisticated software, its purview of possibility is nothing less than unfathomable. Here in this blog, we\u2019ll discuss what exactly is EdTech and what it holds for the education, society and business. Defining EdTech can be elusive because of its vast use case and constant evolution of technology from time to time. However, it\u2019s not the technology but the aim that defines the true essence of EdTech \u2013 utilizing technological resources to improve the education system, pedagogy and ultimately enhancing the learners\u2019 performance. Or as [Investopedia defines](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/edtech.asp): _\u201cEdTech refers to software designed to enhance teacher-led learning in classrooms and improve students\u2019 education outcomes. EdTech is in the early stages of development but it shows promise as a method of customizing the curriculum for a student\u2019s ability level by introducing and reinforcing new content at a pace the student can handle. EdTech is a portmanteau of \u201ceducation\u201d and \u201ctechnology.\u201d_ Let me give you some examples to make things more clear. ## **Some Good Examples of EdTech** ##### **AI and Machine Learning** Artificial Intelligence has already made its mark to boost efficiency and productivity in different industries. Even education is one of them. One of the issues that teachers and educators have been facing for years is providing individual attention to the students in a class of many. Every student in the class has different learning patterns and even a different pace of grasping things. But since we have been following the traditional model of one-size-fits-all education, individual learning is neglected. Here, artificial intelligence and machine learning have solved the problem. AI enables teachers to understand the learning pattern of each student and tailor the learning experience for maximum efficiency. ###### Suggested Read[The World of AI (Artificial Intelligence) Driven Applications](https://www.techuz.com/blog/the-world-of-ai-driven-mobile-applications/)\n...\nTitle: What is EdTech and What it Holds for Education and Startups? The concept here was simple \u2013 teach arithme", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "VR Software Market Size, Growth, Demand & Forecast 2033", "url": "https://www.verifiedmarketreports.com/product/vr-software-market/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: VR Software Market Industry 2025 | U.S Market Forecast & Strategic Trends URL Source: https://www.verifiedmarketreports.com/product/vr-software-market/ Published Time: 2026-03-31T13:33:27+00:00 Markdown Content: ## \ud83d\udcca VR Software Market Snapshot *   **Market Size (2025):**12.41 billion USD *   **Forecast (2033):** 57.55 billion USD *   **CAGR (2026-2033):** 18.68% *   **Primary Applications:** Automotive, Medical, Real Estate, Oil and Gas, Entertainment, Industrial, Other *   **Primary Types:** Basic\uff08$50-150 User/month\uff09, Standard(\uff08$150-225 User/month\uff09), Senior\uff08$225-350/User/month\uff09 *   **Key Regions:** North America (U.S, Canada, Mexico), Europe (Germany, United Kingdom, France), Asia-Pacific (China, Japan, South Korea, India), Middle-East (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Qatar). Source: Verified Market Reports (based on aggregated industry datasets & trade analysis) ## VR Software Market Overview VR Software Market Revenue was valued at 12.41 billion USD in 2024 and is estimated to reach **57.55 billion USD by 2033,** growing at a CAGR of **18.68% from 2026 to 2033.** The VR Software Market encompasses the development, deployment, and management of immersive digital environments designed for virtual reality (VR) hardware platforms. It includes applications across entertainment, enterprise training, healthcare, education, real estate, and simulation-based industries. This market excludes hardware manufacturing, such as VR headsets and peripherals, focusing solely on the software layer that powers experiences, content creation, and platform ecosystems. ![Image 1: VR Software Market  Size And Forecast](https://www.verifiedmarketreports.com/images/01-24/vr-software-market.webp)\n...\nTitle: VR Software Market Industry 2025 | U.S Market Forecast & Strategic Trends URL Source: https://www.verifiedmarketreports.com/product/vr-software-market/ Published Time: 2026-03-31T13:33:27+00:00 Markdown Content: ## \ud83d\udcca VR Software Market Snapshot *   **Market Size (2025):**12.41 billion USD *   **Forecast (2033):** 57.55 billion USD *   **CAGR (2026-2033):** 18.68% *   **Primary Applications:** Automotive, Medical, Real Estate, Oil and Gas, Entertainment, Industrial, Other *   **Primary Types:** Basic\uff08$50-150 User/month\uff09, Standard(\uff08$150-225 User/month\uff09), Senior\uff08$225-350/User/month\uff09 *   **Key Regions:** North America (U.S, Canada, Mexico), Europe (Germany, United Kingdom, France), Asia-Pacific (China, Japan, South Korea, India), Middle-East (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Qatar). It includes applications across enterta", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Career and Top Courses in Data Science. Interesting Statistics", "url": "https://tcglobal.com/careers-and-top-courses-in-data-science/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] URL Source: https://tcglobal.com/careers-and-top-courses-in-data-science/ Published Time: 2021-09-16T07:46:23+00:00 Markdown Content: About Us Our Solutions Events Insights Careers News Testimonials Locations Help Center SCHEDULE A MEETING SIGN UP Careers and Top Courses in Data Science Global Ed Careers and Top Courses in Data Science Libin Thomas Research Scholar Global Ed July 16, 2022 We live in a data-driven world \u2013 every digital action we make translates into data of some sort. Get this \u2013 by 2020, the world had generated around 64.2 zettabytes of data. To put this statistic into perspective, the amount of data that humankind generated right from the beginning of human history until about the mid-2000s was estimated to be around 5 exabytes of data. Do you know how many exabytes make up one zettabyte? One thousand. One exabyte is made up of a thousand petabytes and one petabyte is made up of a thousand terabytes and \u2026 it keeps stacking up. And that\u2019s not the end of it. We have been generating so much data that it is now estimated that we will hit a staggering 180 zettabytes by the end of 2025. But what do we do with all this data? How can we make use of it? The answer is data science. What exactly does it do? Here\u2019s a look Table of contents: Data Science \u2013 the incredible accountant Applications of Data Science Career options in Data Science Top courses in Data Science The future Data science - the incredible accountant In 2013, the world had about 2 billion active Internet users. That number grew to 4.6 billion in 2021. This phenomenal growth in the number of users has been attributed to that one device we just cannot do without \u2013 smartphones. This simply means more data. Most of the data that we create is in the form of streaming, text messages, pictures, and calls. But not all the data that we create is stored. It has been identified that only two percent of the data that we created in 2020 was saved and carried over to 2021. Let\u2019s take a look at some of the interesting statistics related to data. One of the most impressive Google feats is that it processes around 3.5 billion queries on average per day in 2021. (While this article was being written, Google had already processed 7.5 billion queries a day) 5 billion active users on the Internet at the moment. Facebook has around 2.9 billion monthly active users. E-commerce websites saw over 22 billion visits in June 2021\n...\nResearch Scholar Global Ed July 16, 2022 We live in a data-driven world \u2013 every digital action we make translates into data of some sort. To put this statistic into perspective, the amount of data that humankind generated right from the beginning of human history until about the mid-2000s was estimated to be around 5 exabytes of data.
